faqs.question: What causes drain fly infestations in Mount Vernon homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic buildup inside drains, pipes, and sewage systems. In Mount Vernon, older plumbing in historic buildings and homes can accumulate debris that creates ideal breeding conditions. Moisture from leaky pipes, standing water, and insufficient drainage also attract these pests.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Mount Vernon?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and the number of affected drains. Our technicians will inspect all potential breeding sites, treat affected areas, and provide prevention recommendations. Follow-up visits may be scheduled if needed to ensure complete elimination.

faqs.question: Is drain fly treatment safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and methods that are safe for residential use when applied by our licensed professionals. We prioritize eco-friendly solutions and will advise you on any necessary precautions. Most treatments allow you to remain in your home during and immediately after service.

faqs.question: Do you service all neighborhoods in Mount Vernon?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! We provide drain fly treatment throughout all Mount Vernon neighborhoods, including Fleetwood, Chester Heights, Oakley Woods, and the downtown area.
